channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Over 1.5 million pilgrims begin Hajj amid regional tensions
item.description: The spiritual and physical challenge is described by pilgrims as a transformative and deeply meaningful journey.

item.title: How technology is shaping Hajj
item.description: With nearly two million worshippers performing Hajj this year, technology is key to ensuring everything runs smoothly.

item.title: Pope says AI must be ‘disarmed’ to prevent domination, exclusion, and death
item.description: In his first encyclical, Leo insists ownership of artificial intelligence data must not be left solely in private hands.

item.title: Rescuers search for survivors after hotel collapse in Philippines
item.description: Rescuers search for survivors after hotel collapse in Philippines.

item.title: Russia warns foreigners to leave Kyiv as it prepares ‘systematic strikes’
item.description: Moscow calls upcoming 'systematic strikes' on Ukrainian capital a response to Kyiv's recent attack in Luhansk.

item.title: Nearly 300 tourists rescued after cable cars halt mid-air
item.description: Around 300 people were stranded mid-air after the Gondola system suddenly halted in Indian-administered Kashmir.

item.title: Train bomb in Pakistan’s Baloch region: Why violence is on the rise
item.description: Deadly blast in Pakistan marks uptick in Balochistan separatist violence.

item.title: Pope Leo calls for ‘disarming’ of artificial intelligence
item.description: Pope Leo has warned that some AI-powered weapons systems are becoming ‘practically beyond human control'.

item.title: Trump links Iran negotiations to expansion of Abraham Accords
item.description: US President Donald Trump links Iran negotiations to expansion of Abraham Accords.

item.title: Australian Gaza aid flotilla activists allege abuse after Israeli abduction
item.description: Activists allege assaults and torture during Israeli detention after flotilla intercepted in international waters.

item.title: WHO chief says suspected Ebola deaths at 220 as epidemic ‘outpacing us’
item.description: Tedros ⁠Adhanom Ghebreyesus says ​a delay in detecting cases means responders were now 'playing catch-up'.

item.title: Baloch separatists ‘take advantage’ of Pakistan’s entanglements
item.description: Baloch separatists 'take advantage' of Pakistan's entanglements

item.title: Could Israel sabotage US-Iran deal?
item.description: As the US and Iran move closer to a peace deal, Israel vows to keep striking what it calls regional and nuclear threats.

item.title: Iran says deal with US ‘not imminent’ despite progress
item.description: Iran’s FM spokesman Baghaei says a ‘large portion’ of issues with the US have been resolved, but a deal is not imminent.

item.title: Could Brazilian oil emerge as one of the big winners of the Iran war?
item.description: China and India are among those ramping up imports of Brazilian crude amid Strait of Hormuz disruptions.

item.title: What are the biggest FIFA World Cup upsets in history?
item.description: Nothing makes the news like an unfancied or low-ranked team pulling off a shock result at the football World Cup.

item.title: Paraglider survives mid-air collision with plane over Austria
item.description: Paraglider survives mid-air collision with plane over Austria

item.title: Yamal in Spain’s World Cup squad, but no room for Real Madrid players
item.description: Yamal, one of eight Barcelona players named in the 26-man squad, with seven Arsenal players picked by Luis de la Fuente.

item.title: The world urgently needs a US-Iran deal now
item.description: Without a deal between Washington and Tehran, the fallout from Strait of Hormuz closure could deepen global crises.

item.title: China’s Xi praises ‘unbreakable’ Pakistan ties as Sharif visits Beijing
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif says China and Pakistan are 'iron brother' countries.

item.title: Cattle market empties as fear grips Eid preparations in India’s West Bengal
item.description: Tensions rise in key state, won by BJP this month for first time, as political shift disrupts Eid al-Adha traditions.

item.title: Trump’s war loop: Escalate, retreat, repeat
item.description: Stuck in a quagmire of a war, Trump chafes at negative coverage while Iran digs its heels in.

item.title: Surveillance video shows moment of China coal mine blast
item.description: Surveillance video caught the moment a powerful explosion surged through the tunnels of a coal mine in China.

item.title: Israel kills three in attacks on Lebanon, issues more displacement orders
item.description: Israel carries out attacks on multiple villages and towns in southern Lebanon.

item.title: What’s inside the Kaaba and what covers it? The story of the Kiswah
item.description: At the heart of Hajj stands the Kaaba, draped in 670kg of silk and embroidered with 200kg of gold and silver thread.
